summary.intent: Implement a native ReadSkillTool for the moltis skills system, including a prompt-injection safety scanner, gateway registration, prompt_gen rewrite, and integration tests, as specified in a plan file.
summary.outcome: All planned work was completed: ReadSkillTool added to skill_tools.rs, safety.rs module created with prompt-injection scanner, gateway registered the new tool, prompt_gen and system-prompt docs updated, integration tests written and passing, clippy clean on all changed crates, and changes committed.

repo: Skills crate is structured with: types.rs, discover.rs, registry.rs, prompt_gen.rs, lib.rs — safety.rs was a new addition
Gateway tool registration happens in crates/gateway/src/server.rs
System prompt documentation lives in docs/src/system-prompt.md and must be kept in sync with prompt_gen.rs changes
Project uses a 'beads' system for tracking issues/epics; issues are claimed and closed via Bash commands during implementation
There is a known pre-existing macOS CUDA build issue (moltis-csq) that causes top-level `just lint` to fail; per-crate clippy is the workaround
Integration tests for the gateway live in crates/gateway/tests/

code.finding: ReadSkillTool was inserted after DeleteSkillTool and before WriteSkillFilesTool; containment/safety guards pattern mirrors delete/write tools
New module implementing a prompt-injection scanner; exported from lib.rs
Fully rewritten to reflect new native read_skill tool availability; old instruction text referencing SKILL.md reading was removed
Skills-related test around line 1082 was updated to match new prompt_gen output
New integration test file; exercises registered read_skill tool against a seeded skill directory; 5 tests pass

workflow: Per-crate clippy (`cargo clippy -p <crate>`) is the reliable lint approach when top-level just lint fails due to unrelated build issues
Format check produces empty output on success — absence of output means clean
Issues are claimed before starting work and closed after tests pass, providing natural checkpoints
Parallel Bash calls used for independent tasks (claiming issues, running tests on separate crates) to save time

friction: Top-level `just lint` failed due to a pre-existing macOS CUDA build issue (moltis-csq), requiring fallback to per-crate clippy invocations
An unused import warning surfaced after initial compilation of skill_tools.rs and required a follow-up edit
Gateway server.rs needed two edit passes — first edit introduced a compile error that required a second fix before the crate compiled cleanly
ToolRegistry API had to be discovered via grep/bash inspection since it wasn't immediately obvious from existing code
open_items: The plan file (plans/2026-04-10-plan-skills-native-read-tool.md) was checked but not deleted or archived after completion — may be worth cleaning up
Top-level lint via `just lint` remains broken for macOS CUDA builds (moltis-csq); this is a pre-existing issue not addressed in this session
